question_title: Will Solana (SOL) close above $85 on any day between July 11 and July 23, 2026?
summary: SOL needs to close above $85 on at least one day July 11-23, 2026. Two window days are already known (July 11: $76.77, July 12: $76.87), both far below $85. Correcting the anchor to the actual current ~$76.9 (not the stale July-10 $78.07), only 11 days remain and $85 requires a ~10.5% gain. Monte Carlo (vol 3.5-5%, ~0/slightly-negative drift) gives max-close-exceeds-$85 probability ~0.30-0.38. Bearish context (soft BTC, Fear & Greed low, prediction markets favoring $70 support over $90 upside, downtrend into the window) pulls toward the lower-middle. Settle ~0.34.

factors.description: Corrected MC from actual current ~$76.9 over 11 remaining days needing +10.5% to $85; vol 3.5-5% gives P~0.30-0.38
Two in-window days already known below $85 (July 11 $76.77, July 12 $76.87); price trending down into window
13-day (11 remaining) max-over-window materially raises probability vs single-day crossing
Bearish crypto backdrop: soft BTC, low Fear & Greed, prediction markets favor $70 support over $90 upside

reasoning:
**Forecast: 34%**

SOL needs to close above $85 on at least one day in the July 11-23 window. Key correction from the premortem review: two window days are already known ($76.77 and $76.87), both well below $85, and the current price (~$76.9) is below the stale $78.07 July-10 anchor. Modeling the max close over the ~11 remaining days from the actual current price (needing a ~10.5% breakout), Monte Carlo with realistic SOL volatility (3.5-5% daily) and near-zero/slightly-negative drift yields ~0.30-0.38. The bearish crypto backdrop (soft BTC, low Fear & Greed, prediction markets favoring $70 support over $90 upside) pulls toward the lower-middle. Final: **0.34**.
resolution_criteria: This question resolves YES if the daily closing price of Solana (SOL) in US dollars, as reported on the [CoinGecko Solana historical data page](https://www.coingecko.com/en/coins/solana/historical_data), is strictly above $85.00 for at least one calendar day (UTC) from July 11, 2026 through July 23, 2026, inclusive. It resolves NO if every daily "Close" value in that window is $85.00 or lower. If CoinGecko is unavailable, the daily close reported by another major price tracker (e.g. CoinMarketCap) or [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions) will be used.
fine_print: The "Close" column on the CoinGecko historical data page (reported in UTC) is the value used. A close of exactly $85.00 does not count as above the threshold.

factors.description: Max over 13-day window needs to reach $85 (8.9% above $78.07 start); MC with 4.5% daily vol, ~0 drift gives P~0.44
Bearish market tilt: soft BTC (-20% June), prediction markets favor $70 support over $90 upside, negative drift bias
Window is 13 days (max-over-window), which substantially raises probability vs single-day crossing

input.anchor: Random-walk max-over-window base rate: ~0.44 (MC, 4.5% daily vol, zero drift)
input.assessment: Threshold question modeled as max of SOL close over 13 days exceeding $85. Start $78.07, need 8.9% gain. MC across vol 4-5.5% and small drifts gives 0.43-0.50. Bearish context (soft BTC, prediction market bearish tilt) pulls slightly below neutral. Settle ~0.42.
input.tool_audit: sandbox execute_code worked for Monte Carlo. No research tool needed given short-fuse quantitative question with data provided.
input.process_reflection: Straightforward threshold/measurement question. Provided description had enough price data to simulate directly. Scaffolding fine.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
I verified the trace (which contains ONLY the Monte Carlo — the successful web_search from the metrics is not shown) and independently checked SOL price data.

GROUNDING (OK): No fabrication that moves the forecast.
- Factor 2's prediction-market claim is real: CoinGecko shows SOL at ~45.5% for $70 support vs 23.5% for $90 upside — matches "favor $70 support over $90 upside." Bearish sentiment (Fear & Greed ~26, ninth straight red month) is corroborated across sources.
- Factor 1's $78.07 start is a genuine CoinGecko close — but it is the JULY 10 close (a stale anchor; see below).
- All factor logit signs are directionally correct. No double-counting, no wrong-direction factor.

MAIN PROBLEM — stale anchor / ignored known in-window closes (biases P too high):
The question published July 13; the resolution window is July 11–23. As of publication, two window days are ALREADY KNOWN and both are far below $85: July 11 = $76.77 and July 12 = $76.87 (CoinGecko). The agent modeled the entire 13-day window as a fresh random walk starting at $78.07 (the July 10 close). This is wrong in three compounding, same-direction ways:
1. It grants 13 forward days of upside when only ~11 remain unknown from published_at.
2. It starts at $78.07 rather than the actual current ~$76.9, so it needs +8.9% when the real requirement is +10.5% to $85.
3. Price is trending DOWN into the window ($78.07 → $76.9), reinforcing the negative drift the agent only partially applied.

Additionally, the agent assumed 4.5% daily vol, but Yahoo's trailing data and recent daily moves imply closer to ~3.5–4%; the higher figure inflates the probability further. SOL has not closed above ~$82.28 since early July or above $85 since late May, so $85 requires breaking out of the current range.

MY INDEPENDENT ESTIMATE: Correcting the start to ~$76.9 over 11 remaining days with vol 3.5–4.5% and ~0/slightly-negative drift, the max-close-exceeds-$85 probability is ~0.30–0.38, centering ~0.35. The agent's 42% is roughly 7pp too high. This is a real, direction-consistent methodological error, but moderate in magnitude — the agent's bearish tilt already leaned the correct way, so it does not reach the "dramatically wrong" bar for a fail. If the agent were to rebuild the simulation from the actual current price and known window closes, I'd expect ~0.35.

SECONDARY (warn-level): confidence_in_estimate 0.75 is slightly high given the vol input was assumed rather than measured and the known window days were not incorporated. The counterargument is genuine but argues P should be higher (~0.48) — the opposite direction of the actual error. Tool audit omits that the research call 100%-errored, though it doesn't falsely claim "no failures."

Verdict: warn — grounded factors and correct direction, but the model anchors on a stale July-10 price and ignores already-resolved in-window closes, biasing the probability ~7pp high. Recommend rerunning the MC from S0≈$76.9 over the 11 remaining days.
